report_name: Booking.com Breach Exposes Traveler Data, Fueling Fears of Targeted Scams

threat_actors.name: Unidentified Attackers
threat_actors.description: Unauthorized third parties who gained access to Booking.com partner (hotel) accounts to steal customer reservation data for use in sophisticated phishing campaigns.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Attackers targeted hotel staff with phishing emails to steal their login credentials for the Booking.com partner portal.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used stolen credentials for hotel partner accounts to log into the Booking.com platform and access guest reservation data.

attack_patterns.name: Masquerading
attack_patterns.description: Attackers crafted fraudulent messages that perfectly mimicked official communications from Booking.com or the associated hotel, using stolen data to make them appear legitimate to the victims.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: Attackers accessed and collected customer reservation data, including names, contact details, and booking specifics, from the Booking.com system via compromised partner accounts.

identities.name: Booking.com
identities.description: A global online travel agency whose platform was compromised, leading to the exposure of customer reservation data.

infrastructure.name: Compromised Booking.com Partner Portal
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled hotel partner accounts on the Booking.com portal, used to access guest reservation data and send fraudulent messages.

indicators.name: Anomalous Booking.com Reservation Data Export
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Booking.com Reservation Data Export
id: 2b9c1f8a-3e4d-4b1a-8f9c-1d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Monitors for usage of API endpoints that export customer data, which could indicate a compromised partner account exfiltrating reservation details as described in the Booking.com breach.
references:
  - https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/04/14/booking-com-data-breach/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-14
modified: 2026-04-14
tags:
  - attack.t1213
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains: '/api/reservations/export'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative actions or automated systems that export reservation data.
  - Rule may need tuning based on normal traffic patterns and user roles.
indicators.description: Detects access to API endpoints used for exporting reservation data, a pattern indicative of the data collection phase of the Booking.com breach.
